# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.

Use safe data-only formats and validate the resulting structure/types before use.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

class MarshalSessionStore < ActionDispatch::Session::AbstractStore
  def get_session(env, sid)
    cookies = env['HTTP_COOKIE']
    return {} unless cookies && cookies.include?('session')
    
    begin
      session_data = Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies['session']))
      validate_session_data(session_data)
    rescue => e
      Rails.logger.warn "Invalid session data: #{e.message}"
      {}
    end
  end

  def set_session(env, sid, session_data, options)
    return unless session_data
    
    begin
      validate_session_data(session_data)
      serialized_data = Base64.encode64(Marshal.dump(session_data))
      cookie_options = {
        value: serialized_data,
        expires: options[:expire_after] ? Time.current + options[:expire_after] : nil,
        httponly: true,
        secure: options[:secure],
        same_site: options[:same_site]
      }
      env['rack.session.options'] = cookie_options
    rescue => e
      Rails.logger.warn "Failed to serialize session data: #{e.message}"
    end
  end

  def destroy_session(env, sid, options)
    env['rack.session.options'] = {
      value: '',
      expires: Time.current - 1.day,
      httponly: true,
      secure: options[:secure],
      same_site: options[:same_site]
    }
  end

  private

  def validate_session_data(data)
    return if data.nil?
    
    unless data.is_a?(Hash)
      raise ArgumentError, "Session data must be a Hash"
    end
    
    data.each do |key, value|
      unless key.is_a?(String) || key.is_a?(Symbol)
        raise ArgumentError, "Session keys must be String or Symbol"
      end
      
      unless valid_session_value?(value)
        raise ArgumentError, "Invalid session value type: #{value.class}"
      end
    end
  end

  def valid_session_value?(value)
    return true if value.nil?
    
    case value
    when String, Numeric, TrueClass, FalseClass, Array, Hash, Date, Time
      true
    when Symbol
      false
    else
      false
    end
  end
end
